evals.prompt: Generate a resume for this job: https://careers.google.com/jobs/results/123456-software-engineer/
evals.expected_output: Reads .claude/resume-config.json, fetches JD via WebFetch, extracts company/position/requirements/tech stack/keywords, reads all material files, presents strategy with relevance mapping and ATS keywords, waits for approval, generates .tex files following template conventions, compiles PDFs, saves jd.md

evals.prompt:
Create a CV for this position:

Senior ML Engineer at Stripe
Requirements: 5+ years Python, PyTorch, distributed systems
Nice to have: Payments domain knowledge, Scala
evals.expected_output: Accepts raw JD text, extracts fields (company: Stripe, position: Senior ML Engineer, etc.), reads materials, presents strategy, generates tailored .tex + .pdf in docs/resumes/stripe-senior-ml-engineer/

evals.prompt: Generate resume for Appier ML Engineer --skip-strategy
evals.expected_output: Skips the strategy approval step, auto-determines strategy from JD vs materials, proceeds directly to template learning and generation

evals.prompt: Generate a resume for this job posting: https://example.com/jobs/404
evals.expected_output: Attempts WebFetch, URL fails or returns no JD content, informs the user that the URL couldn't be fetched, asks the user to paste the JD text directly instead of silently failing

evals.prompt: Create a CV for Senior Engineer at Google
evals.expected_output: Detects that .claude/resume-config.json is missing, tells the user to run /setup-resume first, does NOT attempt to generate without configuration

evals.prompt: Generate resume for Stripe payments engineer --cv-only
evals.expected_output: Generates only the CV .tex file, skips cover letter generation entirely, compilation produces only CV PDF
